prs.title: ui: improve dashboard visual consistency with accent border styling
improve: refine churn feature importance visualization
feat: implement Streamlit UI for churn prediction system
feature: add inline color picker next to hex code in canvas background settings
prs.body:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8347d3b2-d5ee-4ac2-b9ea-60e23364f563

### Summary
This PR adds a compact, inline color picker next to the Hex code input in the Canvas background settings panel. This improves usability by allowing users to visually choose any color instead of typing hex values manually.

### What’s Changed
- Added a native `<input type="color">` next to the hex input field.
- Synced the picker with the hex input: selecting a color updates the text field and canvas background.
- Existing swatches and behavior remain unchanged.
- Updated snapshot tests to reflect the new UI.

### Why
Previously, users could:
- choose preset swatches
- type a custom hex value manually

But there was no UI for easily picking a custom color visually.  
This enhancement keeps Excalidraw simple while improving convenience.

### Testing
- Ran `yarn test -u` to update snapshots.
- All tests pass (`1032 passed, 0 failed`).
- Verified canvas background updates correctly from:
  - hex input
  - new color picker
  - existing swatches

Closes #10457

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Color Picker UI for Canvas Background
issues.body: **Current Behavior** The Canvas Background menu provides a few preset colors and a hex input field, but no visual color picker. **Problem** Users who don’t know hex codes must guess or switch tools to find colors. This makes picking a custom canvas background inconvenient. **Proposed Solution** Add a small, lightweight color picker (e.g., a hue/saturation panel or native ``) that appears when choosing a custom color. **Why This Helps** - Easier for users to pick any color visually - Improves UX without complicating the UI - Fully optional (existing presets and hex input remain) **My Contribution** I would like to implement this feature and have prepared a PR with the basic color picker integration.
